destinations.evidence: encode_supported_origin_tx uses encode_2718 for Legacy, Eip1559, and Passkey origin transactions, but uses tx.tx().encode_for_signing for TransactionSigned::Scheduled.
destinations.snippet:
Legacy(tx) => tx.encode_2718(&mut out),
Eip1559(tx) => tx.encode_2718(&mut out),
Scheduled(tx) => tx.tx().encode_for_signing(&mut out),
Passkey(tx) => tx.encode_2718(&mut out),

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port payload async path: ordinary supported origins are encoded as EIP-2718 bytes while scheduled origins are special-cased through encode_for_signing, preserving scheduled origin-byte hash semantics.
rationale: The packet contains a direct conflict: an older scheduled-hash candidate claims full signed EIP-2718 hashing, while later/current evidence uses encode_for_signing for scheduled recalculation and scheduled async originals. The refined invariant keeps the current special case.
statement: Async detection uses EIP-2718 bytes for ordinary typed origin transactions but uses scheduled signing-payload bytes for scheduled originals, matching the current scheduled transaction hash semantics.
suggested_falsification_tests: Detect async calls for an EIP-1559 origin and assert origin_tx_rlp begins with the EIP-2718 type byte and decodes through the EIP-2718 decoder.
Detect async calls for a Scheduled origin and assert stored origin bytes equal encode_for_signing output and not the full signed EIP-2718 encoding.
Recalculate a signed scheduled transaction hash and assert it matches the signing-payload hash path used by scheduled transaction construction.
summary: Scheduled origin-byte semantics are special-cased through encode_for_signing.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Origin-byte encoding regression tests for scheduled and ordinary async origins.
title: Scheduled origin-byte semantics are special-cased
